raw_text:
Unofficial Translation by the Insurance Regulator of Cambodia

- Revocation of the approval or the registration;
- Restriction on the insurance agent professional activities.

**Article 19 ..**

Shall be deemed as forgery of public document and shall be punishable in accordance with Criminal Code for any individual insurance agent who violates Article 12 of this Prakas.

**Article 20 ..**

Shall be subject to administrative sanctions as stipulated in Article 18 of this Prakas for any individual insurance agent who exceeds the number of insurance company allowed as set out in Article 13 of this Prakas.

**Article 21 ..**

Formalities and procedures of administrative sanctions and penalties stipulated in this Chapter shall be determined by the **I.R.C..**

Any person directly affected by the decision of the **I.R.C.** as stated in this Chapter may bring a complaint in accordance with the procedures stipulated in the Law on Insurance.

**Article 22 ..**

Administrative sanctions stated in this Chapter may not restrain the applicability of criminal law and may not compensate any other damages.

**Chapter 5**

**Other provision**

**Article 23 ..**

Individual insurance agents who are currently serving as insurance agents shall be excepted from the requirement to provide at least high school diploma for the registration in individual insurance agent register as stated in the Article 6 of this Prakas and may replace the high school diploma with an at least 2-year (two) working expericnese as an insurance agent certification letter from a partnered-insurance company.

**Chapter 6**

**Transitional provision**

**Article 24 ..**

Within 6 (six) months from the effective date of this Prakas, an individual who has served as insurance agent in the Kingdom of Cambodia shall apply for registration in individual insurance agent register of **I.R.C.** in accordance with the formalities and procedures as set out in this Prakas.

**Chapter 7**

**Final Provisions**

**Article 25.-**

Any provision contrary to this Prakas shall be abrogated.
